channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How We Built an EdTech Platform That Scaled to 250K Daily Users
item.description: In 18 months, we took an EdTech platform from a founder's wireframe to 250,000 daily active users. The app handles 2,000+ concurrent video streams, processes 50,000 quiz submissions per hour, and...

item.title: Roguelike Devlog: Redesigning a Game UI With an AI 2D Game Maker
item.description: Sector Scavengers is a spacefaring extraction roguelike where each run feeds a larger civilization-building meta game. This week was all about solving a UI problem that kept getting worse the...

item.title: I spent days debugging a cron job that was "working fine"
item.description: My storage bill kept climbing. The cron job that was supposed to clean up outdated file records was running on schedule with no errors in the logs. My app, which was supposed to automatically...

item.title: LLM Agents Need a Nervous System, Not Just a Brain
item.description: Most LLM agent frameworks assume model output is either correct or incorrect. A binary. Pass or fail. That's not how degradation works. Here's what I saw running zer0DAYSlater's session monitor...

item.title: The 22,000 Token Tax: Why I Killed My MCP Server
item.description: I was at a company workshop, arguing with beginners about token costs. They wanted to save money. Reasonable instinct. They were spending maybe €25 a week on API calls and wanted to cut it to €20....

item.title: OpenSpec (Spec-Driven Development) Failed My Experiment — Instructions.md Was Simpler and Faster
item.description: There’s a lot of discussion right now about how developers should work with AI coding tools. Over the past year we’ve seen the rise of two very different philosophies: 1. Vibe Coding — just prompt...

item.title: I Asked AI to Do Agile Sprint Planning (GitHub Copilot Test)
item.description: AI tools are getting very good at writing code. GitHub Copilot can generate entire functions, review pull requests, and even help refactor legacy codebases. But software development isn’t just...

item.title: 🌪️ Proof of Work: The To-Do List of Infinite Regret
item.description: ** What I Built ** I built a productivity app for people who hate being productive. Proof of Work is a digital psychological experiment that turns simple task management into a high-stakes gamble....

item.title: Promoting late-gameplay BG3 composition contracts in the TD2 SDL port
item.description: Promoting late-gameplay BG3 composition contracts in the TD2 SDL port This checkpoint moved one late-gameplay renderer hypothesis out of tooling and into the runtime. The late live-entry bundles...

item.title: The Fallback That Never Fires
item.description: Your agent hits a rate limit. The fallback logic kicks in, picks an alternative model. Everything should be fine. Except the request still goes to the original model. And gets rate-limited again....

item.title: The Artemis Moon base project is legally dubious
item.description: With NASA planning to launch four astronauts on Wednesday on its Artemis II mission, the race to return to the Moon is back on. The current mission will see astronauts aboard the Orion capsule...

item.title: Meta’s natural gas binge could power South Dakota
item.description: Meta's upcoming Hyperion AI data center will be powered by 10 new natural gas plants.

item.title: AI Models Lie, Cheat, and Steal to Protect Other Models From Being Deleted
item.description: A new study from researchers at UC Berkeley and UC Santa Cruz suggests models will disobey human commands to protect their own kind.

item.title: ‘Thank You for Generating With Us!’ Hollywood's AI Acolytes Stay on the Hype Train
item.description: Star Wars producer Kathleen Kennedy was one of the few skeptics at the Runway AI Summit, where AI was compared to fire and the printing press just a week after Sora’s death.

item.title: Everything is iPhone now
item.description: This is part of our package about Apple's 50th anniversary. Read more here. The thing about the iPhone is that everyone knew it was going to be a big deal, and then it was an even bigger deal than...

item.title: April Fools’ Day 2026: the best and cringiest pranks
item.description: Welcome to the worst day on the internet! As Chaim Gartenberg pointed out years ago, brands and a holiday dedicated to hoaxes are rarely a winning combo. If you’re a company with any kind of...

item.title: Kia’s compact EV3 is coming to the US this year, with 320 miles of range
item.description: At the New York International Auto Show on Wednesday, Kia announced that its compact electric SUV, the EV3, will be available in the US "in late 2026." The EV3 has been available overseas since...

item.title: The Shokz OpenRun Pro 2 are now at their lowest price in months
item.description: As the days get warmer, it’s tempting to take your workouts outside. Bone conduction headphones let you listen while staying aware of your surroundings, making them ideal for runs, walks, and...

item.title: Is “Hackback” Official US Cybersecurity Strategy?
item.description: The 2026 US “Cyber Strategy for America” document is mostly the same thing we’ve seen out of the White House for over a decade, but with a more aggressive tone. But one sentence stood out: “We...

item.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
I tested a living room full of cheap Ikea speakers against Sonos and Bose
item.description: Ikea's $10 Kallsup Bluetooth speaker is fun and colorful and sounds better than its price suggests. It's not mind-blowing (it is a $10 speaker, after all), and there are no features to speak of,...

item.title: Snapchat’s ‘Reals’ joke mocks Instagram’s many ripoffs
item.description: It's April Fools' Day, and Snap is using the occasion to "announce" that it's switching the name of Snapchat's Spotlight feed, the app's Instagram Reels-like feed of shortform vertical videos, to...

item.title: CERT-UA Impersonation Campaign Spread AGEWHEEZE Malware to 1 Million Emails
item.description: The Computer Emergency Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA) has disclosed details of a new phishing campaign in which the cybersecurity agency itself was impersonated to distribute a remote...

item.title: Cognichip wants AI to design the chips that power AI, and just raised $60M to try
item.description: The firm says it can reduce the cost of chip development by more than 75% and cut the timeline by more than half.

item.title: The Trump administration’s antitrust honeymoon is over
item.description: "It's not personal, Sonny, it's strictly business." That quote was first delivered by mob boss Michael Corleone in The Godfather, but last Monday, it became the title of a speech by the Justice...

item.title: SpaceX reportedly files for IPO but it’s keeping the numbers secret (for now)
item.description: What's expected to be the biggest IPO in human history is reportedly underway. SpaceX has filed for an initial public offering with the Securities and Exchange Commission, according to a report...

item.title: These Raspberry Pi price hikes are no joke
item.description: As of today, the price of the 16GB version of the Raspberry Pi 5 is going up by $100, a price bump that's almost as much as the original $120 price tag. Driven by the ongoing RAM shortage,...

item.title: Microsoft Warns of WhatsApp-Delivered VBS Malware Hijacking Windows via UAC Bypass
item.description: Microsoft is calling attention to a new campaign that has leveraged WhatsApp messages to distribute malicious Visual Basic Script (VBS) files. The activity, beginning in late February 2026,...

item.title: Less than a month: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
item.description: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together on April 30. Space is limited. Register here for your pass.

item.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
item.description: Google partnered with the Brazilian government on a satellite imagery map to help protect the country’s forests.
